name: Algarve, Portugal

visa.notes: D7 visa requires proof of passive income. NHR ended Jan 2024; IFICI replacement excludes pensions; standard progressive rates 14.5%-48%.

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: SNS (Serviço Nacional de Saúde) - public universal healthcare with private option. Residents access public system via health center registration.
healthcare.waitTimes: Moderate for specialists (2-6 weeks), shorter in Algarve due to lower population density
healthcare.prescriptionCoverage: Public system covers most prescriptions with co-pays of 0-90% depending on category

pros: Large established expat community
Warm year-round climate with 300+ days of sunshine
Affordable cost of living by Western European standards
cons: Tourist-heavy areas can feel less authentic

taxes.notes: NHR ended Jan 2024. IFICI replacement excludes pensions. Standard progressive rates 14.5%-48%. US-Portugal tax treaty helps avoid double taxation.

name: Porto, Portugal

visa.notes: D7 visa requires proof of passive income. NHR ended Jan 2024; IFICI replacement excludes pensions.

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: SNS with Hospital de São João as major public hospital. Good private options via CUF and Lusíadas.
healthcare.waitTimes: Moderate (3-6 weeks for specialists)
healthcare.prescriptionCoverage: Public system covers most prescriptions with tiered co-pays

pros: More affordable than Lisbon with similar amenities
World-famous wine region (Port wine)
UNESCO World Heritage riverside district
cons: Cooler and rainier than southern Portugal

taxes.notes: NHR ended Jan 2024. IFICI replacement excludes pensions. Standard progressive rates 14.5%-48%.

name: Silver Coast, Portugal

visa.notes: D7 visa requires proof of passive income. NHR ended Jan 2024; IFICI replacement excludes pensions.

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: SNS public healthcare. Caldas da Rainha has a regional hospital. Larger facilities accessible in Lisbon (1 hour).
healthcare.waitTimes: Longer waits in smaller towns (4-8 weeks for specialists)
healthcare.prescriptionCoverage: Standard SNS prescription coverage with co-pays

pros: Very affordable cost of living
Beautiful coastline with surfing culture
Close to Lisbon (1 hour) while maintaining small-town feel
cons: Smaller expat community than Algarve or Lisbon
Cooler and windier than southern Portugal

taxes.notes: NHR ended Jan 2024. IFICI replacement excludes pensions. Standard progressive rates 14.5%-48%.

name: Cascais, Portugal

visa.notes: D7 visa requires proof of passive income. NHR ended Jan 2024; IFICI replacement excludes pensions.

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: SNS public healthcare plus excellent private clinics. Easy access to Lisbon hospitals (30 min).
healthcare.waitTimes: Moderate (3-6 weeks for specialists); fast private options nearby
healthcare.prescriptionCoverage: Standard SNS coverage with co-pays; private insurance widely used

pros: Upscale beach town with excellent quality of life
Train connection to Lisbon (35 min)
Strong international community and English widely spoken
cons: One of the most expensive areas in Portugal

taxes.notes: NHR ended Jan 2024. IFICI replacement excludes pensions. Standard progressive rates 14.5%-48%.

name: Costa del Sol, Spain

visa.notes: Non-lucrative visa requires proof of sufficient funds (~€2,400/mo). Cannot work in Spain on this visa.

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: Spanish public healthcare (SAS in Andalusia) is excellent. Private insurance (Sanitas, Adeslas) widely available and affordable.
healthcare.waitTimes: Public: 2-8 weeks for specialists. Private: days to 2 weeks.
healthcare.prescriptionCoverage: Public covers most prescriptions with small co-pays; pensioners pay reduced rates

pros: Massive established British and international expat community
320+ days of sunshine per year
Excellent healthcare infrastructure in Málaga
cons: Tourist-heavy areas can feel like enclaves

taxes.notes: Spanish progressive income tax 19%-47%. Beckham Law may apply for new residents (flat 24% up to €600K). US-Spain tax treaty.

name: Valencia, Spain

visa.notes: Non-lucrative visa requires proof of sufficient funds (~€2,400/mo). Cannot work in Spain on this visa.

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: Valencian public health system (Conselleria de Sanitat) with modern hospitals. Private options plentiful.
healthcare.waitTimes: Public: 3-8 weeks for specialists. Private: under 2 weeks.
healthcare.prescriptionCoverage: Public covers most prescriptions with co-pays based on income

pros: Excellent value for a major Mediterranean city
City of Arts and Sciences, great cultural scene
Good metro and bus system, bikeable
cons: Less English spoken than Costa del Sol
Flash flooding risk in autumn (DANA events)

taxes.notes: Spanish progressive income tax 19%-47%. Valencia region may have slightly different regional rates.

name: Barcelona, Spain

visa.notes: Non-lucrative visa requires proof of sufficient funds (~€2,400/mo). Cannot work in Spain on this visa.

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: CatSalut (Catalan public health) with world-class hospitals like Hospital Clínic. Extensive private options.
healthcare.waitTimes: Public: 4-10 weeks for specialists. Private: under 2 weeks.
healthcare.prescriptionCoverage: Public covers most prescriptions; retirees get reduced co-pays

pros: World-class culture, architecture, food, and nightlife
Excellent metro and public transit system
Beach city with Mediterranean climate
cons: High cost of living, especially rent
Pickpocketing and petty crime in tourist areas

taxes.notes: Spanish progressive income tax 19%-47%. Catalonia has slightly higher regional surcharges.

name: Canary Islands, Spain

visa.notes: Non-lucrative visa requires proof of sufficient funds (~€2,400/mo). Cannot work in Spain on this visa.

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: Spanish SCS (Servicio Canario de Salud) with good hospitals in major cities. Air evacuation to mainland for complex cases.
healthcare.waitTimes: Public: 3-10 weeks for specialists. Private options more limited than mainland.
healthcare.prescriptionCoverage: Standard Spanish prescription coverage with co-pays

pros: Year-round warm and mild climate (eternal spring)
Lower IGIC tax (7%) instead of mainland VAT (21%)
Large Northern European expat community
cons: Island isolation - flights to mainland are 2.5+ hours
Limited shopping and cultural options vs mainland
Some goods more expensive due to shipping costs

taxes.notes: Spanish progressive income tax 19%-47%. Canary Islands use IGIC (7%) instead of mainland IVA/VAT (21%).

name: Athens, Greece

visa.notes: Financially independent person visa requires ~€2,000/mo income proof. Renewable annually.

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: EOPYY national health system. Public hospitals adequate but strained. Private clinics good and affordable.
healthcare.waitTimes: Public: 4-12 weeks for specialists (system under strain). Private: 1-2 weeks.
healthcare.prescriptionCoverage: Public covers most prescriptions with co-pays of 10-25%

pros: Rich ancient history at your doorstep
Very affordable dining and daily costs
Good public transit (metro, buses, tram)
cons: Air pollution can be poor in summer
Bureaucracy can be challenging for residents

taxes.notes: Greek progressive income tax 9%-44%. Special 7% flat tax for foreign retirees transferring tax residency (10-year program). US-Greece tax treaty.

name: Crete, Greece

visa.notes: Financially independent person visa requires ~€2,000/mo income proof.

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: Regional hospital in Heraklion (PAGNI) is largest on the island. Limited specialist care may require travel to Athens.
healthcare.waitTimes: Public: 4-12 weeks. Complex cases may need Athens referral.
healthcare.prescriptionCoverage: Standard EOPYY coverage with co-pays

pros: Very affordable cost of living
Warm climate with beautiful beaches and mountains
Excellent Cretan cuisine and food culture
cons: Island isolation - ferry or flights to mainland

taxes.notes: Greek progressive income tax 9%-44%. 7% flat tax option for foreign retirees.

name: Corfu, Greece

visa.notes: Financially independent person visa requires ~€2,000/mo income proof.

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: Corfu General Hospital for basic care. Specialist treatment may require travel to Athens or Thessaloniki.
healthcare.waitTimes: Public: 4-12 weeks. Complex cases need mainland referral.
healthcare.prescriptionCoverage: Standard EOPYY coverage with co-pays

pros: Lush green Ionian island (unlike drier Aegean islands)
Venetian and British architectural heritage
Affordable and peaceful year-round lifestyle
cons: Very quiet in winter (many businesses close)

taxes.notes: Greek progressive income tax 9%-44%. 7% flat tax option for foreign retirees.

name: Rhodes, Greece

visa.notes: Financially independent person visa requires ~€2,000/mo income proof.

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: Rhodes General Hospital provides basic and emergency care. Specialist treatment may require Athens travel.
healthcare.waitTimes: Public: 4-12 weeks. Complex cases need mainland referral.
healthcare.prescriptionCoverage: Standard EOPYY coverage with co-pays

pros: Very warm climate with 300+ days of sunshine
Medieval old town is a UNESCO World Heritage site
Affordable cost of living
cons: Island isolation with seasonal flight connections

taxes.notes: Greek progressive income tax 9%-44%. 7% flat tax option for foreign retirees.

name: Peloponnese, Greece

visa.notes: Financially independent person visa requires ~€2,000/mo income proof.

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: Regional hospitals in Patras and Kalamata. Basic care adequate; specialist care may require Athens travel (2-3 hours).
healthcare.waitTimes: Public: 4-12 weeks. Smaller towns have longer waits.
healthcare.prescriptionCoverage: Standard EOPYY coverage with co-pays

pros: Among the most affordable areas in the EU
Rich ancient history (Mycenae, Olympia, Epidaurus)
Authentic Greek mainland experience without mass tourism
cons: Limited English spoken outside tourist spots

taxes.notes: Greek progressive income tax 9%-44%. 7% flat tax option for foreign retirees.

name: Languedoc, France

visa.notes: Long-stay visa requires ~€1,500/mo income. Must apply at French consulate. Renewable annually.

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: French Sécurité Sociale (Assurance Maladie) - world-class system. Residents get carte vitale after 3 months. CHU Montpellier is a major university hospital.
healthcare.waitTimes: GPs: same week. Specialists: 2-6 weeks. Shorter in Montpellier.
healthcare.prescriptionCoverage: 70% covered by Sécu, remainder by mutuelle (supplemental insurance)

pros: Warm Mediterranean climate in southern France
Affordable by French standards
Montpellier is a vibrant university city with tram system
cons: French language essential for daily life
Smaller international community than Côte d'Azur

taxes.notes: French progressive income tax 11%-45%. Social charges ~9.1% on foreign income (CSG/CRDS). US-France tax treaty.

name: Dordogne, France

visa.notes: Long-stay visa requires ~€1,500/mo income. Must apply at French consulate.

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: French Sécurité Sociale. Rural area with good local clinics; larger hospitals in Périgueux and Bordeaux (1.5 hours).
healthcare.waitTimes: GPs: same week. Specialists: 3-8 weeks. May need Bordeaux for complex care.
healthcare.prescriptionCoverage: 70% covered by Sécu, remainder by mutuelle

pros: Stunning countryside with medieval villages and castles
World-class food culture (foie gras, truffles, wine)
Very affordable housing with large properties available

taxes.notes: French progressive income tax 11%-45%. Social charges ~9.1% on foreign income.

name: Gascony, France

visa.notes: Long-stay visa requires ~€1,500/mo income. Must apply at French consulate.

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: French Sécurité Sociale. Rural area with local clinics; Toulouse (1-1.5 hours) for specialist care.
healthcare.waitTimes: GPs: same week. Specialists: 4-10 weeks. Toulouse for complex care.
healthcare.prescriptionCoverage: 70% covered by Sécu, remainder by mutuelle

pros: Among the most affordable areas in France
Outstanding food and wine culture (Armagnac, duck)
Peaceful rural lifestyle with friendly locals
cons: Small expat community, can feel isolated

taxes.notes: French progressive income tax 11%-45%. Social charges ~9.1% on foreign income.

name: Nice / Côte d'Azur, France

visa.notes: Long-stay visa requires ~€1,500/mo income. Must apply at French consulate.

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: French Sécurité Sociale. CHU Nice (Pasteur 2) and multiple private clinics. Excellent specialist care.
healthcare.waitTimes: GPs: same week. Specialists: 2-6 weeks. Private options fast.
healthcare.prescriptionCoverage: 70% covered by Sécu, remainder by mutuelle

pros: Glamorous Mediterranean lifestyle with stunning scenery
International airport with worldwide connections
Large international community and English widely understood
cons: Very high cost of living, especially rent

taxes.notes: French progressive income tax 11%-45%. Social charges ~9.1% on foreign income.

name: Paris, France

visa.notes: Long-stay visa requires ~€1,500/mo income. Must apply at French consulate.

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: French Sécurité Sociale. AP-HP is the largest hospital system in Europe. World-class specialist care available.
healthcare.waitTimes: GPs: same day to 1 week. Specialists: 1-4 weeks. Best access in France.
healthcare.prescriptionCoverage: 70% covered by Sécu, remainder by mutuelle

pros: Unmatched cultural offerings (museums, theater, cuisine)
World-class public transit (metro, RER, buses)
International city with huge expat community
cons: Very high cost of living, especially rent

taxes.notes: French progressive income tax 11%-45%. Social charges ~9.1% on foreign income.

name: Toulon, France

visa.notes: Long-stay visa requires ~€1,500/mo income. Must apply at French consulate.

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: French Sécurité Sociale. Hôpital Sainte Musse and military hospital (HIA Sainte-Anne). Good specialist access.
healthcare.waitTimes: GPs: same week. Specialists: 2-6 weeks.
healthcare.prescriptionCoverage: 70% covered by Sécu, remainder by mutuelle

pros: Mediterranean climate at significantly lower cost than Nice
French naval city with authentic character
Access to Porquerolles and Hyères islands
cons: Less glamorous and fewer amenities than Nice/Cannes
Some rougher neighborhoods in the city center

taxes.notes: French progressive income tax 11%-45%. Social charges ~9.1% on foreign income.

name: Abruzzo, Italy

visa.notes: Elective residence visa requires ~€31K/year income. Must demonstrate passive income and health insurance.

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: Italian SSN (Servizio Sanitario Nazionale). Regional hospital in Pescara. Quality varies but adequate for most needs.
healthcare.waitTimes: Public: 4-12 weeks for specialists. Private affordable alternative.
healthcare.prescriptionCoverage: SSN covers essential prescriptions with tiered co-pays (ticket)

pros: Among the most affordable regions in Italy
Both mountains and coast within 30 minutes
Authentic Italian lifestyle without mass tourism

taxes.notes: Italian progressive income tax 23%-43%. Italy offers 7% flat tax for retirees moving to southern regions (towns under 20K population). US-Italy tax treaty.

name: Puglia, Italy

visa.notes: Elective residence visa requires ~€31K/year income.

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: Italian SSN. Policlinico di Bari for major care. Rural areas have less coverage.
healthcare.waitTimes: Public: 4-12 weeks. Private clinics in Bari and Lecce offer faster care.
healthcare.prescriptionCoverage: SSN covers essential prescriptions with tiered co-pays

pros: Beautiful Baroque architecture (Lecce is 'Florence of the South')
Excellent food and wine at very low prices
Stunning coastline and trulli houses in Ostuni/Alberobello
cons: Limited English spoken outside tourist areas
Southern Italian bureaucracy can be slow

taxes.notes: Italian progressive income tax 23%-43%. 7% flat tax for retirees in southern towns under 20K population.

name: Sicily, Italy

visa.notes: Elective residence visa requires ~€31K/year income.

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: Italian SSN. Major hospitals in Palermo and Catania. Quality improving but historically underfunded vs northern Italy.
healthcare.waitTimes: Public: 6-16 weeks. Private options available in major cities.
healthcare.prescriptionCoverage: SSN covers essential prescriptions with tiered co-pays

pros: Among the cheapest places to live in Western Europe
Incredible food, history, and natural beauty
Warm climate with mild winters
cons: Infrastructure and bureaucracy can be challenging
Organized crime still present in some areas

taxes.notes: Italian progressive income tax 23%-43%. 7% flat tax for retirees in southern towns under 20K population.

name: Tuscany, Italy

visa.notes: Elective residence visa requires ~€31K/year income.

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: Italian SSN. Excellent hospitals in Florence (AOU Careggi) accessible from most Tuscan towns. Good specialist care.
healthcare.waitTimes: Public: 3-10 weeks. Private clinics widely available.
healthcare.prescriptionCoverage: SSN covers essential prescriptions with tiered co-pays

pros: Iconic rolling hills, vineyards, and Renaissance culture
Strong expat community in smaller towns like Lucca
Excellent food and wine at every price point
cons: Higher cost of living than southern Italy

taxes.notes: Italian progressive income tax 23%-43%. 7% flat tax not available in most Tuscan towns (population over 20K).

name: Sardinia, Italy

visa.notes: Elective residence visa requires ~€31K/year income.

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: Italian SSN. AOU Cagliari for major care. Limited specialist care may require mainland travel.
healthcare.waitTimes: Public: 4-12 weeks. Complex cases may need mainland referral.
healthcare.prescriptionCoverage: SSN covers essential prescriptions with tiered co-pays

pros: Stunning beaches rivaling Caribbean quality
Warm Mediterranean climate
Unique Sardinian culture and cuisine
cons: Island isolation - flights or ferry to mainland
Limited English and smaller expat scene

taxes.notes: Italian progressive income tax 23%-43%. 7% flat tax may apply in qualifying small towns.

name: Italian Lake Region, Italy

visa.notes: Elective residence visa requires ~€31K/year income.

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: Italian SSN. Proximity to Milan (1-1.5 hours) gives access to world-class hospitals. Local hospitals adequate for routine care.
healthcare.waitTimes: Public: 3-10 weeks. Milan private options easily accessible.
healthcare.prescriptionCoverage: SSN covers essential prescriptions with tiered co-pays

pros: Spectacularly scenic lakefront living
Close to Milan and Swiss border for shopping and travel
Mild microclimate (warmer than surrounding areas due to lakes)
cons: Higher cost of living than southern Italy

taxes.notes: Italian progressive income tax 23%-43%. 7% flat tax not available (northern Italy, larger towns).

name: Galway, Ireland

visa.notes: Stamp 0 visa for retirees requires ~€50K/year income. Must have private health insurance.

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: HSE with University Hospital Galway. Good general care; specialists may require Dublin travel.
healthcare.waitTimes: Public: 6-18 months. Private: 2-6 weeks.
healthcare.prescriptionCoverage: Private insurance required for Stamp 0 holders

pros: Vibrant arts, music, and pub culture
Stunning scenery (Connemara, Aran Islands nearby)
English-speaking with welcoming community
cons: Higher rents due to university town demand

taxes.notes: Irish progressive income tax 20%/40%. USC 0.5-8%. US-Ireland tax treaty.

name: Cork, Ireland

visa.notes: Stamp 0 visa for retirees requires ~€50K/year income. Must have private health insurance.

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: HSE with Cork University Hospital (CUH) - one of Ireland's largest hospitals. Good specialist care.
healthcare.waitTimes: Public: 6-18 months. Private: 2-6 weeks.
healthcare.prescriptionCoverage: Private insurance required for Stamp 0 holders

pros: Ireland's food capital with English Market and restaurant scene
Growing tech hub with Apple, EMC, and others
Good airport with European connections
cons: Public transit limited compared to Dublin

taxes.notes: Irish progressive income tax 20%/40%. USC 0.5-8%. US-Ireland tax treaty.

name: Limerick, Ireland

visa.notes: Stamp 0 visa for retirees requires ~€50K/year income. Must have private health insurance.

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: HSE with University Hospital Limerick. Adequate general care; some specialties require Cork or Dublin.
healthcare.waitTimes: Public: 6-18 months. Private: 2-6 weeks.
healthcare.prescriptionCoverage: Private insurance required for Stamp 0 holders

pros: Most affordable major Irish city
Gateway to Wild Atlantic Way and Clare
Strong rugby culture and community spirit
cons: Some areas have reputation for anti-social behavior
Fewer cultural amenities than Cork or Galway

taxes.notes: Irish progressive income tax 20%/40%. USC 0.5-8%. US-Ireland tax treaty.

name: Wexford, Ireland

visa.notes: Stamp 0 visa for retirees requires ~€50K/year income. Must have private health insurance.

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: HSE with Wexford General Hospital. Basic care adequate; Dublin (1.5 hours) for specialist care.
healthcare.waitTimes: Public: 6-18 months. Private: 2-6 weeks.
healthcare.prescriptionCoverage: Private insurance required for Stamp 0 holders

pros: Sunniest county in Ireland
Affordable by Irish standards
Beautiful beaches and historic Norman heritage

taxes.notes: Irish progressive income tax 20%/40%. USC 0.5-8%. US-Ireland tax treaty.

name: Dubrovnik, Croatia

visa.notes: Temporary stay permit for retirees/financially independent persons. Digital nomad visa also available. Croatia joined Schengen and euro in 2023.

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: HZZO (Croatian Health Insurance Fund). Dubrovnik General Hospital for basic care. Specialist care may require Split or Zagreb.
healthcare.waitTimes: Public: 4-12 weeks. Private clinics available in city.
healthcare.prescriptionCoverage: HZZO covers most prescriptions with small co-pays

pros: Stunning medieval walled city on the Adriatic
Excellent seafood and Mediterranean lifestyle
Croatia in EU and Schengen since 2023
cons: Higher cost than rest of Croatia

taxes.notes: Croatian progressive income tax 20% (up to €50,400) and 30% above. Surtax may apply (0-18% depending on municipality). No US-Croatia tax treaty.

name: Split, Croatia

visa.notes: Temporary stay permit for financially independent persons. Digital nomad visa also available.

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: HZZO with KBC Split (Clinical Hospital Center Split) - second largest hospital in Croatia. Good specialist care.
healthcare.waitTimes: Public: 4-12 weeks. Private clinics available.
healthcare.prescriptionCoverage: HZZO covers most prescriptions with small co-pays

pros: Diocletian's Palace - living in a Roman monument
Good ferry connections to islands
More affordable than Dubrovnik with better daily infrastructure
cons: Winter can feel quiet and grey

taxes.notes: Croatian progressive income tax 20%/30%. Municipal surtax may apply. No US-Croatia tax treaty.

name: Istria, Croatia

visa.notes: Temporary stay permit for financially independent persons. Digital nomad visa also available.

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: HZZO with Pula General Hospital. Italian-influenced region with decent care; Zagreb or Rijeka for complex cases.
healthcare.waitTimes: Public: 4-12 weeks. Private clinics available in Pula.
healthcare.prescriptionCoverage: HZZO covers most prescriptions with small co-pays

pros: Italian-influenced cuisine with excellent truffles and wine
Affordable with charming coastal towns
Close to Italy and Slovenia for day trips
cons: Car essential for exploring the peninsula

taxes.notes: Croatian progressive income tax 20%/30%. Municipal surtax may apply. No US-Croatia tax treaty.

name: Zagreb, Croatia

visa.notes: Temporary stay permit for financially independent persons. Digital nomad visa also available.

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: HZZO with KBC Zagreb - Croatia's largest and best hospital. Full specialist care available.
healthcare.waitTimes: Public: 3-10 weeks. Private: 1-2 weeks.
healthcare.prescriptionCoverage: HZZO covers most prescriptions with small co-pays

pros: Very affordable European capital
Best healthcare in Croatia
Good public tram and bus system
cons: No coast (2+ hours to the sea)
Earthquake damage (2020) still being repaired in some areas

taxes.notes: Croatian progressive income tax 20%/30%. Zagreb has 18% municipal surtax. No US-Croatia tax treaty.

name: Paphos, Cyprus

visa.notes: Category F permit requires ~€9,568/year + €4,613/dependent. Must deposit €15K-€30K in Cypriot bank. Very retiree-friendly.

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: GESY (General Healthcare System) introduced 2019. Paphos General Hospital for basic care. Private clinics good and affordable.
healthcare.waitTimes: Public: 2-8 weeks for specialists. Private: under 2 weeks.
healthcare.prescriptionCoverage: GESY covers most prescriptions with small co-pays

pros: Massive British expat community, English widely spoken
Warm year-round climate with 340 days of sunshine
Very favorable tax treatment for retirees (2.65% on pensions)

taxes.notes: Cyprus: flat 2.65% on pension income for first €3,420, then 0%. Or opt for standard progressive rates 20%-35%. Very favorable for retirees. US-Cyprus tax treaty.

name: Limassol, Cyprus

visa.notes: Category F permit requires ~€9,568/year + €4,613/dependent.

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: GESY public system plus strong private sector. Limassol General Hospital. Good quality care.
healthcare.waitTimes: Public: 2-8 weeks. Private: under 2 weeks.
healthcare.prescriptionCoverage: GESY covers most prescriptions with small co-pays

pros: Cosmopolitan business hub with international restaurants
Strong Russian and British expat communities
New marina and waterfront development
cons: Higher cost than other Cypriot cities

taxes.notes: Cyprus: flat 2.65% on pension income for first €3,420, then 0%. Very favorable for retirees.

name: Larnaca, Cyprus

visa.notes: Category F permit requires ~€9,568/year + €4,613/dependent.

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: GESY public system. Larnaca General Hospital for basic care. Specialist care may require Nicosia or Limassol.
healthcare.waitTimes: Public: 2-8 weeks. Private: under 2 weeks.
healthcare.prescriptionCoverage: GESY covers most prescriptions with small co-pays

pros: Most affordable Cypriot city
International airport for easy travel
Relaxed beach town atmosphere
cons: Smaller and less cosmopolitan than Limassol

taxes.notes: Cyprus: flat 2.65% on pension income for first €3,420, then 0%. Very favorable for retirees.

name: Valletta, Malta

visa.notes: Malta Retirement Programme requires property purchase/rental + €14,318/year income. 15% flat tax on remitted income, minimum €7,500/year tax.

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: Mater Dei Hospital (public, free for residents). Good quality. Small island means all healthcare is nearby.
healthcare.waitTimes: Public: 2-8 weeks for specialists. Private: under 2 weeks.
healthcare.prescriptionCoverage: Public healthcare provides free prescriptions for residents on approved list

pros: English is an official language
Warm year-round Mediterranean climate
Stunning historic capital (UNESCO World Heritage)
cons: Very small and densely populated island
Limited green spaces and dog-walking areas

taxes.notes: Malta Retirement Programme: 15% flat tax on remitted income, min €7,500/year. Standard rates 0%-35% otherwise. US-Malta tax treaty.

name: Sliema, Malta

visa.notes: Malta Retirement Programme requires property purchase/rental + €14,318/year income. 15% flat tax on remitted income, minimum €7,500/year tax.

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: Mater Dei Hospital nearby. Many private clinics in Sliema area. Good English-speaking medical staff.
healthcare.waitTimes: Public: 2-8 weeks. Private: under 2 weeks.
healthcare.prescriptionCoverage: Public healthcare provides free prescriptions on approved list

pros: Modern waterfront area popular with expats
Walkable with shops, restaurants, and promenade
English widely spoken everywhere
cons: Construction boom causing noise and disruption
Higher rents than rest of Malta

taxes.notes: Malta Retirement Programme: 15% flat tax on remitted income, min €7,500/year.

name: Gozo, Malta

visa.notes: Malta Retirement Programme requires property purchase/rental + €14,318/year income. Gozo qualifies for reduced property requirements.

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: Gozo General Hospital for basic care. Mater Dei Hospital on Malta main island (30 min ferry) for specialist care.
healthcare.waitTimes: Public: 2-8 weeks. Complex cases require ferry to Malta main island.
healthcare.prescriptionCoverage: Public healthcare provides free prescriptions on approved list

pros: Very affordable by Maltese standards
Quieter, more rural island lifestyle
English spoken, very safe, warm climate
cons: Dependent on ferry to Malta for specialist healthcare and shopping
Can feel isolated, especially in winter

taxes.notes: Malta Retirement Programme: 15% flat tax on remitted income, min €7,500/year. Gozo has reduced property requirements.
